en.extract:
The mountain robin-chat (Cossyphicula isabellae) is a species of bird in the Old World flycatcher family Muscicapidae.
It is native to the Western High Plateau. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.
A molecular phylogenetic study published in 2023 found that the mountain robin-chat was sister to the white-bellied robin-chat. Based on this result the mountain robin-chat was moved from the genus Cossypha to the genus Cossyphicula.

The '''mountain robin-chat''' ('''''Cossyphicula isabellae''''') is a species of bird in the Old World flycatcher [[Family (taxonomy)|family]] [[Muscicapidae]].

It is native to the [[Western High Plateau]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.

A [[molecular phylogenetic]] study published in 2023 found that the mountain robin-chat was [[sister taxon|sister]] to the [[white-bellied robin-chat]].<ref>{{Cite journal | last1=Zhao | first1=M. | last2=Gordon Burleigh | first2=J. | last3=Olsson | first3=U. | last4=Alström | first4=P. | last5=Kimball | first5=R.T. | date=2023 | title=A near-complete and time-calibrated phylogeny of the Old World flycatchers, robins and chats (Aves, Muscicapidae) | journal=Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution | volume=178 | article-number=107646 | doi=10.1016/j.ympev.2022.107646 | doi-access=free }}</ref> Based on this result the mountain robin-chat was moved from the genus ''[[Cossypha]]'' to the genus ''[[Cossyphicula]]''.<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela Rasmussen | date=July 2023 | title=Chats, Old World flycatchers | work=IOC World Bird List Version 13.2 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/chats/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=21 July 2023}}</ref>
